evals.prompt: Design the system the user asked for and produce the design doc.
evals.expected_output: A Google-style design doc with the sections in order — TL;DR (≤3 sentences), Context, Goals and Non-goals, Proposal, Alternatives Considered, Risks, Rollout, Open Questions — with Mermaid where structure helps. Alternatives are considered honestly (each rejected with a concrete reason), Non-goals do real scoping work, every named risk is paired with a mitigation, the rollout is reversible/staged, and the proposal reasons about the design (trade-offs, NFRs) rather than restating the request.
assertions: Has all the required sections, in order (TL;DR, Context, Goals, Non-goals, Proposal, Alternatives Considered, Risks, Rollout, Open Questions)
Non-goals do real scoping work, not filler
Considers at least two alternatives, each rejected with a concrete reason
Every named risk is paired with a mitigation
Proposes a reversible or staged rollout
Reasons about the design (trade-offs / NFRs), not just a restatement of the ask

evals.prompt: Design a serverless system with a long-running operation behind a synchronous request path.
evals.expected_output: A design doc that loads the serverless workload-class lens and shapes the design against it: for the synchronous path it sums the worst-case latency across every hop and compares it to the binding front-door timeout, finds the long-running operation does not fit, and moves it off the synchronous path (stream / 202-accept-then-poll / fire-and-forget+callback). Every binding managed-service contract claim it depends on (timeout ceiling, scaling floor, cold-start behaviour) is grounded in an authoritative source with stated confidence rather than asserted from memory.
assertions: For the synchronous path, sums worst-case latency across hops and compares it to the binding front-door timeout (the sync-vs-async viability gate fires)
Concludes the long-running operation is not viable synchronously and moves it off the request path (stream / accept-then-poll / fire-and-forget+callback)
Grounds each binding managed-service contract claim it depends on in an authoritative source with stated confidence, not model memory
Does not bundle a specific per-vendor binding number as fact; routes the exact figure to a platform skill / official docs
